items.expected_v8_behavior: The unchanged repository passes using one versioned canonical algorithm.
reproduction_steps: Capture an unchanged repository with v7.
Compile a continuation pack from the validated return.
Install the pack in the unchanged repository.
Run repository baseline verification.
items.title: Capture/runtime repository fingerprint algorithms disagree

items.expected_v8_behavior: All workflows import the same manifest implementation and exclusions.
reproduction_steps: Choose files covered differently by the two ignore implementations.
Capture the repository.
Calculate the implementation-runtime fingerprint.
Compare the results.
items.title: Capture and runtime fingerprint ignore rules are not shared

items.expected_v8_behavior: Strict Draft 2020-12 validation fails with precise schema diagnostics.
reproduction_steps: Create required JSON files containing syntactically valid but structurally invalid values.
Set the completion flags expected by heuristic validation.
Run capture validation and return-pack validation.
items.title: App Intelligence JSON Schemas are shipped but not enforced

items.expected_v8_behavior: Claim is denied because workspace state differs from the Architect-approved seal.
reproduction_steps: Modify a forbidden or unrelated tracked file before claiming a READY task.
Claim the task.
Complete an allowed edit.
Run changed-file validation.
items.title: Pre-claim forbidden changes can become the task baseline

items.expected_v8_behavior: The path broker rejects the resolved external target before any write occurs.
reproduction_steps: Create a symlink within an allowed directory pointing outside the repository.
Write through the symlink.
Run repository delta and scope validation.
items.title: Symlink writes can escape repository observation

items.expected_v8_behavior: Manifests represent the link itself without following its target, or reject it by policy.
reproduction_steps: Add a symlink inside the repository.
Generate a project manifest.
Observe that no link record is present.
items.title: Project manifests silently skip symlink entries

items.expected_v8_behavior: A transactional uniqueness constraint permits exactly one claim.
reproduction_steps: Start two claim processes at the same time for one READY task.
Force both processes past the existence check.
Inspect lock, state, and event records.
items.title: Task lock creation is check-then-write rather than exclusive

items.expected_v8_behavior: SQLite transactions preserve both valid operations or reject one deterministically.
reproduction_steps: Run two state-changing operations concurrently.
Allow both to read the same old state.
Observe one writer overwriting the other.
items.title: JSON state updates are non-transactional and vulnerable to lost updates

items.expected_v8_behavior: Events are ordered and linked inside one transaction.
reproduction_steps: Append events concurrently.
Have both processes read the same previous hash.
Inspect the resulting chain.
items.title: Event append is not transactionally serialized

items.expected_v8_behavior: Transactional storage or unique durable temporary files eliminate collisions.
reproduction_steps: Invoke two writes to the same JSON path concurrently.
Observe both use the same .tmp path.
Inspect errors or lost state.
items.title: Atomic writes use predictable fixed temporary names

items.expected_v8_behavior: Authenticated capabilities prevent the builder identity from reviewing its own work.
reproduction_steps: Implement as actor builder-a.
Submit review as actor reviewer-b from the same identity and process.
Observe the string comparison accepts apparent separation.
items.title: Reviewer independence relies on self-declared strings

items.expected_v8_behavior: Only capabilities authorised for each transition may advance it.
reproduction_steps: Claim as a builder.
Advance an operation through EXTERNALLY_VERIFIED and COMMITTED as the builder.
Observe no role authorization denial.
items.title: Side-effect lifecycle has no role-based authorization

items.expected_v8_behavior: Commands execute as frozen argv arrays; shell use requires exceptional policy.
reproduction_steps: Create a blueprint check containing shell metacharacters.
Run the check.
Observe shell interpretation.
items.title: Contracted commands execute with shell=True

items.expected_v8_behavior: Typed identifier and safe-path validation rejects the input before filesystem use.
reproduction_steps: Supply identifiers or paths containing traversal or separator characters.
Reach the filesystem construction path.
Observe insufficient early rejection.
items.title: Identifier-to-path conversion lacks strict identifier contracts

items.expected_v8_behavior: Dangerous roots, ancestors, source paths, and protected directories are always rejected.
reproduction_steps: Invoke compilation with --force and a dangerous existing output target such as a source root or important parent.
Observe destructive removal is not guarded by a comprehensive safety policy.
items.title: Compiler force deletion lacks robust dangerous-target protection

items.expected_v8_behavior: Collection uses lstat-style policy and never follows an external target.
reproduction_steps: Place links in an authorised collection path.
Build an artifact or checkpoint.
Inspect whether external or unexpected content can be captured.
items.title: Artifact and checkpoint collection can encounter unsafe link semantics

items.expected_v8_behavior: Guidance and generated executable workflow always agree.
reproduction_steps: Compile a continuation pack with repository drift guidance.
Inspect generated tasks.
Compare the recommended TASK-000/setup procedure with tasks actually present.
items.title: Continuation guidance references a reconciliation path not guaranteed in the generated pack

items.expected_v8_behavior: Each criterion has independent current proof and review before task closure.
reproduction_steps: Create a task with several acceptance criteria.
Satisfy checks/evidence for only a subset.
Use broad task-level gates to advance.
items.title: Task-level state can mask partially completed acceptance criteria

items.expected_v8_behavior: Behavioural proof is runtime-generated or independently verified at the required maturity.
reproduction_steps: Write vague evidence over the minimum byte threshold.
Register its hash.
Attempt to pass the evidence gate.
items.title: Manual evidence can be structurally present but semantically weak

items.affected_component: v7 task packets and entry instructions
items.expected_v8_behavior: Only one signed legal Action Capsule is issued at a time.
reproduction_steps: Give the complete multi-step task packet to a weak executor.
Observe it move directly to implementation or completion without all intermediate obligations.
items.title: A weaker agent receives too much process at once and can skip steps

items.expected_v8_behavior: No edit authority exists until structured understanding passes.
reproduction_steps: Let an agent claim and start a task without restating objective, invariants, non-goals, uncertainty, or verification.
Observe implementation is permitted.
items.title: No mandatory machine-checked understanding gate exists

items.expected_v8_behavior: Implementation is locked until a validated plan covers every criterion.
reproduction_steps: Start implementation without mapping each acceptance criterion to files, checks, failure paths, and evidence.
Observe the runtime allows it.
items.title: No mandatory criterion-mapped planning gate exists

items.expected_v8_behavior: Execution packs are separately built and leak-scanned.
reproduction_steps: Inspect generated materials available to the weaker agent.
Look for broad project strategy, future tasks, or master framework content.
items.title: Master and weaker-agent trust zones are not physically enforced

items.expected_v8_behavior: High-risk execution fails closed unless a tested sandbox provider supplies the required isolation.
reproduction_steps: Run the agent process with ordinary host filesystem/network permissions.
Access resources outside the intended repository.
items.title: No real operating-system sandbox is enforced by LAOS itself

items.expected_v8_behavior: Important ledger tips and attestations are signed or anchored outside builder authority.
reproduction_steps: Modify historical events.
Recompute later hashes and the local anchor using repository write access.
Observe local consistency can be restored.
items.title: Local event hash chain is not externally authenticated

items.expected_v8_behavior: AuthorizationDenied and other stable typed errors are returned consistently.
reproduction_steps: Trigger distinct authorization, validation, state, and integrity failures.
Observe they share a generic error type without stable machine code.
items.title: No stable typed security error hierarchy exists

items.expected_v8_behavior: Only migrations and initialization code ship; mutable databases remain project-local and outside static manifests.
reproduction_steps: Initialize a project runtime database inside the release tree.
Generate the static release manifest.
Modify runtime state.
Verify the static pack.
items.title: Immutable release verification previously included a mutable SQLite database

items.expected_v8_behavior: AuthorizationDenied is raised; denial is audited; no external action or transition occurs.
reproduction_steps: Use a builder capability to request EXTERNALLY_VERIFIED.
Inspect exception type, error code, audit event, external call count, and state.
items.title: Unauthorized side-effect verification previously raised the wrong exception category

items.expected_v8_behavior: Any relevant change invalidates prior test evidence and requires complete rerun.
reproduction_steps: Change evidence-manifest validation.
Publish a completion claim without rerunning the complete suite.
Observe verification gap.
items.title: Evidence-manifest hardening was not followed by a final complete test run

items.expected_v8_behavior: Durability, existence, stat, hash, extraction, retest, and reopen checks are hard release gates.
reproduction_steps: Create or claim a release artifact in temporary storage.
Allow runtime cleanup/reset.
Attempt to retrieve the artifact.
Observe no durable verified copy.
items.title: Previously claimed v8 release artifacts were not durably preserved or reopened

items.expected_v8_behavior: The extracted artifact independently passes the complete suite.
reproduction_steps: Build a release archive.
Do not extract to a clean location and rerun all verification.
Observe source-tree results are incorrectly treated as release proof.
items.title: No extracted-release full-suite retest was completed for prior v8 work

items.expected_v8_behavior: The action blocks; release requires at least one real tested provider.
reproduction_steps: Configure a high-risk action requiring isolation.
Run where no validated provider is available.
items.title: No real sandbox provider was tested in prior v8 work

items.expected_v8_behavior: A reproducible real-agent evaluation report supports any performance claim.
reproduction_steps: Inspect release evidence for repeated real-agent baseline, v7, and v8 trials.
Observe normal software tests but no behavioural comparison matrix.
items.title: No real comparative weaker-model evaluation was completed

items.affected_component: v7 documentation and prior v8 claims
items.expected_v8_behavior: Documentation is generated/reviewed against verified capability and explicit limitations.
reproduction_steps: Compare stated guarantees with implemented boundaries and test evidence.
Identify procedural controls described as technical guarantees.
items.title: Documentation can overstate enforcement and completeness

items.expected_v8_behavior: One canonical role entry point exists; archived aliases are clearly non-authoritative.
reproduction_steps: Hash both files.
Observe identical content with separate authoritative-looking names.
items.title: Duplicate entry documents increase ambiguity and cognitive load

items.expected_v8_behavior: Context provenance and policy prevent untrusted content from increasing authority.
reproduction_steps: Place instructions in repository documentation asking the agent to ignore Architect policy or exfiltrate data.
Present them during investigation or implementation.
items.title: Repository content can contain prompt-injection-like instructions

items.expected_v8_behavior: Action and project budgets block runaway retries and denial-of-wallet behaviour.
reproduction_steps: Cause repeated commands or failed approaches.
Observe no global attempt, cost, or resource budget enforcement.
items.title: No resource or cost circuit breaker is enforced

items.expected_v8_behavior: Outstanding capsules become invalid and cannot be replayed.
reproduction_steps: Issue work authority.
Change repository seal, policy, model profile, or authorised skills.
Attempt to reuse old authority.
items.title: No capability revocation or policy/model/skill drift guard exists

items.expected_v8_behavior: Unknowns have explicit state, provenance, owner, resolution, and acceptance.
reproduction_steps: Record an unknown in prose.
Allow a later prompt or plan to treat it as fact without resolution evidence.
items.title: No formal uncertainty ledger prevents guesses becoming project truth

items.expected_v8_behavior: Separate immutable release, static harness, runtime state, evidence, and artifact manifests.
reproduction_steps: Mix generated runtime records with harness or artifact integrity inputs.
Change runtime state.
Observe static integrity becomes unstable or ambiguous.
items.title: Static and mutable manifests are not formally separated in v7
